Kamakura, Japan · April 20, 2026

dAIly digest

Issue №2 · Vol. 1 · compiled in 00:00:25

☞

AI agents ship design tools, browser harnesses, and Shopify skills as Claude 4.7 tests accelerate.

Claude Code April 18, 2026

Browser Harness — a self-healing browser framework

Browser Harness is an open-source library that lets LLMs drive Chrome directly over CDP WebSocket with no intermediate framework. It includes a self-healing system that edits helper Python functions on-the-fly when tasks fail.

Why it mattersDrop-in alternative to Playwright/Puppeteer wrappers when you need a browser that adapts to the page instead of the other way around — useful for agent-driven scraping and QA pipelines Mike already runs.

via X

The Morning's Dispatch

99 items saved today

  1. Claude Code April 17, 2026

    /autofix-pr — Cursor's CI-loop command from the terminal

    /autofix-pr is a Cursor command that lets you trigger the cloud-based Autofix PR workflow directly from your terminal after pushing a branch. It uploads your full session context so the autofixer can resolve CI failures, address review comments, and iterate without manual intervention.

    Why it mattersCloses the loop on a PR in one CLI step instead of switching to the web UI, tightening the local-to-cloud dev workflow.

    via X

  2. Claude Code April 16, 2026

    Local token-spend dashboard parsing Claude Code logs

    A developer built a local dashboard that parses the log files Claude Code already writes to disk, surfacing per-model and per-project token usage that Anthropic's official UI deliberately hides.

    Why it mattersPrecise observability into token spend without a black-box vendor dashboard — directly useful when wiring Claude into your own agents.

    via X

  3. Design April 15, 2026

    Drams — a Rams-inspired Framer component library

    A free collection of beautifully crafted, interactive Framer components built with strict adherence to Dieter Rams' ten principles of good design.

    Why it mattersProduction-ready, skeuomorphic UI assets that drop straight into Framer prototypes or serve as high-fidelity reference.

    via X

  4. Design April 14, 2026

    Clone any site's rendered DOM with Claude + Chrome MCP

    A Claude-powered skill that uses Chrome's MCP to inspect a live website's rendered DOM, fonts, colors, and layout, then generates a pixel-perfect clone in a single prompt.

    Why it mattersPractical pattern for letting an LLM read the browser's live computed styles instead of guessing from screenshots.

    via X

  5. AI April 17, 2026

    Claude Design by Anthropic Labs — prototypes by talking

    A new Anthropic Labs product lets you collaborate with Claude Opus 4.7 to create polished prototypes, slides, and one-pagers through conversation.

    Why it mattersDirect reference for how Anthropic positions voice-driven design work — useful when shaping your own AI-augmented dev UX.

    via X

  6. Shopify April 17, 2026

    63 Shopify Admin API agent skills, open-sourced

    Open-source repo of 63 pre-built Claude/Code/Cursor agent skills connecting directly to the Shopify Admin API — cart recovery, inventory auditing, revenue reports, bulk price updates, more.

    Why it mattersBattle-tested MCP-style tools that drop into your AI workflows and save days of GraphQL scaffolding.

    via X

✦

Old Gold

From the archive — earlier saves resurfaced because they speak to today

  1. OAuth 2.0 PKCE demystified — a walk-through

    A careful, diagram-heavy explainer of the OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow — the extension that makes public clients (mobile apps, CLIs, SPAs) safe without a shared secret.

    ↺ Thematic match — today's pieces on MCP auth and Claude Code skills point right back to the PKCE post you saved last summer while wiring up the first version of Mame.

    via X saved 251 days ago

  2. "Fewer tools = more reliable agent"

    A short manifesto on LLM agent design: the fewer tools you give a model, the more reliably it will use them.

    ↺ Directly echoes today's Shopify-skills piece — both argue that agent quality compounds inversely with API surface area.

    via X saved 202 days ago

99 saved today · 99 in archive · top: Claude Code

content.text:
Homepage is now magazine-front structured:

**Top-to-bottom flow:**
1. **Masthead** — newspaper rule + dAIly wordmark + issue meta (unchanged)
2. **Signal epigraph** — daily headline, centered (unchanged)
3. **Featured** — full-width-ish top story with big hero image (max-height 56vh), category tag + date, display-serif title (Bodoni up to 3.2rem — well above the 2rem threshold), full summary with drop cap in accent red, inline why-it-matters, source link
4. **"The Morning's Dispatch"** header — with item count
5. **Responsive grid** — 2 columns on desktop (auto-fit minmax 22rem), 1 on mobile. Each card has optional hero thumbnail (16:9), category tag + date, Hanken title, full summary, why-it-matters, source. Source link pinned to bottom for consistent grid alignment.
6. **Old Gold** — same gilded treatment as before, kept as its own section below the main grid
7. **Colophon**

**Design decisions worth flagging:**
- Category tags render in accent red. Today all items will show empty categories (since everything's `(unfiled)` — I wired `{{#unless (eq folder "(unfiled)")}}` so the tag hides); once folder categorization lands, the grid lights up with real tags.
- Featured gets the drop cap (moved from "first item of first dispatch" to "featured summary")
- Grid items are text-first, image optional — the mix in the preview (some with hero, some without) is intentional demonstration
- Handlebars note: I used `(eq ...)` and `{{#unless}}` helpers — the Phase 4 renderer will need Handlebars or equivalent; a plain-string renderer would need to implement these. Flagging for Phase 4.

The heavy lifting is done. Take a look — biggest open call is whether the featured-hero at 56vh feels right on a laptop (it dominates the fold) or if you want it smaller to show more grid above the scroll.
